This study analyzed primary HMIs for intelligent electric vehicles (EVs) using established guidelines, industry standards, and the Electric Vehicle UI Design Specification. The 15 items were categorized into five factors: Factor 1: Layout mode, Factor 2: Interactive mode, Factor 3: Color mode, Factor 4: Feedback mode, Factor 5: Prompt model.

Full_Reference_Text: This iterative process identified the most effective HMI configurations. The GA applied evolutionary processes to a population of HMI designs, with fitness evaluated based on DNN predictions. After 185 iterations, the optimal individual was identified with a preference score of approximately 5.570. The simulation involved driving on a straight, two-lane city road for 5 min at a speed of 30 km/h. Participants included 32 university students (18 males, 14 females) aged 19 to 25, all specializing in product design.

Reference_Text_Preview: we use machine learning-based surrogate models to predict the performances of car shapes based on th...
Full_Reference_Text: we use machine learning-based surrogate models to predict the performances of car shapes based on the low-dimensional representation learned by 3D point cloud (variational) autoencoders. Furthermore, we exploit the stochastic nature of the representation learned by variational autoencoders to augment the training data for our surrogate models. We utilize the regularized latent space of a variational point cloud autoencoder for fast design prototyping and data augmentation.

Article_Title: 167_Optimizing HMI for Intelligent Electric Vehicles Using BCI and Deep Neural Networks with Genetic Algorithms
Reference_Text_Preview: This study utilizes a brain—computer interface (BCI)—based deep neural network (DNN) and genetic alg...
Full_Reference_Text: This study utilizes a brain—computer interface (BCI)—based deep neural network (DNN) and genetic algorithm (GA) method. The deep neural network (DNN) topology linking HMI visual coding to e-Sense prediction comprises three layers: an input layer with 26 neurons, three fully connected hidden layers, and an output layer with one neurone. The network utilized rectified linear unit (ReLU) activation functions across the hidden layers, with a regression layer at the output. Deep neural networks (DNNs) play a key role in the personalized human-machine interaction interfaces (HMIs) of intelligent electric vehicles. By analyzing complex user data, interactions are customized, the learning environment is optimized, and the user experience is enhanced.

Article_Title: 172_Beyond Manual Modeling Automating GUI Model Generation Using Design Documents
Reference_Text_Preview: Throughout the development lifecycle of a GUI application, various artifacts are produced, with UI/U...
Full_Reference_Text: Throughout the development lifecycle of a GUI application, various artifacts are produced, with UI/UX designs being particularly significant alongside source code, especially in the industrial production environments of large companies. UI/UX designs are crucial as they define the intended appearance and behavior of the application before implementation. Model-based testing (MBT) is an effective approach for this purpose. MBT uses abstract models to represent the expected behavior of the GUI, helping identify and address issues to maintain high-quality user experiences.
Notes: The reference text combines content from two separate locations: the first portion about UI/UX designs in development lifecycle appears on Page 2, while the MBT description appears on Page 1. Article_Title: 167_Optimizing HMI for Intelligent Electric Vehicles Using BCI and Deep Neural Networks with Genetic Algorithms
Reference_Text_Preview: This research identified essential HMI elements for intelligent electric vehicles to understand user...
Full_Reference_Text: This research identified essential HMI elements for intelligent electric vehicles to understand user interaction needs. The developed DNN-GA analyzed user behavior patterns from EEG data to predict preferences and attention levels during driving. These predictions guided the GA in optimizing HMI layouts. In this experiment, EEG data were measured using an AR system integrated into a real vehicle cockpit environment, providing an immersive and realistic driving experience. Participants sequentially interacted with the AR HMI prototype's main control interface, performing tasks such as adjusting the rearview mirror, turning on driving lights, adjusting the seat, closing the sunshade, activating the 360° driving camera, turning on music, and, finally, opening the map. This setup ensured a realistic and interactive evaluation of the HMI design.
Notes: The latter portion of the reference text (starting from 'In this experiment, EEG data were measured...') appears word-for-word on Page 9. However, the first three sentences about 'identifying essential HMI elements' and 'DNN-GA analyzed user behavior patterns' are NOT directly quoted from the excerpt - these appear to be synthesized summaries of the methodology described across pages 10-12.

Reference_Text_Preview: In this paper, a generative product design method, KENPI method, is proposed, which mainly includes ...
Full_Reference_Text: In this paper, a generative product design method, KENPI method, is proposed, which mainly includes mapping model construction, style transfer model construction, and transfer result analysis. BP neural network is used to construct the nonlinear mapping model of product modeling element space and product perceptual semantic space. The trained model is used to predict the product semantics in content maps and guide the selection of style maps. A generative innovative design method of KENPI products is proposed, which can automatically identify, extract, and reconstruct the content and color features of images, and transfer them to the product modeling in the content map to realize the real-time generation of new product images.

Article_Title: 136_The Generative Generic-Field Design Method Based on Design Cognition and Knowledge Reasoning
Reference_Text_Preview: Based on the topic model, a statistical model widely used in semantic analysis and text mining, Conc...
Full_Reference_Text: Based on the topic model, a statistical model widely used in semantic analysis and text mining, ConceptTM, is proposed to generate a set of physical structures containing implicit connections for a given technical field and functional requirements. ConceptTM adopts the PGM, especially the infrastructure of the topic model, to systematically express the complex and implicit correlations among physical structures. The modeling thinking of the probabilistic topic model is to regard the text as the probability distribution of the topic; that is, each text is composed of several topics, and the topic is regarded as the probability distribution of words.
